text: Last class you proposed an idea and asked for approval.
Today a plan goes wrong, and you handle the problem on a call.

If the part is missing, we will order a new one.
If we ship today, it will arrive on Monday.
If they confirm by Friday, we will keep the schedule.
I can look into it, and I will fix it today.
Andreia, the team is upset. There is a complaint about the delay. It is urgent.
I understand. First, I apologize for the inconvenience. Let me explain the solution.
Good. What happens if the supplier cannot give us a firm date?
If they cannot confirm a date, we will ask for a replacement unit as a backup.
That is a clear plan. How will you calm the surgeons?
I will reassure them: if practice stops, we will use the backup, so no real surgery is affected.
I apologize for the delay and the inconvenience.
Let me reassure you: we have a backup plan.

Hi Andreia, David Lewis here. I am sorry to call with a problem. The supplier just told me the new console will be delayed by two weeks, and the training team is not happy. One of the surgeons already filed a complaint, because we promised the equipment for this month. I know this is urgent, so here is what I need. Please call the supplier and find out the real reason for the delay. If the part is missing, ask when it will ship. If they cannot give us a firm date, we will ask for a replacement unit on loan. Above all, apologize to the team and reassure them that we have a backup plan. Offer a clear solution and a new date. If you handle this calmly, the team will trust us again. Thank you, Andreia. Call me after you speak to them.

Good afternoon, everyone. I want to update you on the console, and first, I apologize for the delay and the inconvenience. Let me explain the problem and the solution. The supplier had a missing part, so the unit is delayed by two weeks. I know this is urgent for the training team. Here is the plan. If the new date slips again, we will use a loan unit as a backup, so practice will not stop. I have asked the supplier for a written commitment. If they confirm by Friday, we will keep the original schedule. I want to reassure you that no real surgery is affected. If anyone is still worried, please come and talk to me. We will fix this together, and I will send a short update every week. Thank you for your patience.

Let me describe how Andreia handles the complaint call. First, she apologizes for the delay and the inconvenience. Then she explains the problem: the supplier had a missing part. Next, she offers a solution with the first conditional: if the date slips again, we will use a backup unit. After that, she reassures the team that no real surgery is affected. Finally, she promises a clear new date and a weekly update.
